top_findings.summary: The skill "bir-tax-filing" was presented only as a file path (`../../docs/claude-code-skills/community/bir-tax-filing`) without any actual source code content. This prevents analysis for malicious patterns. In a supply chain security context, skills that hide or obfuscate their source code are inherently suspicious, as legitimate skills should have transparent, analyzable code. Without code, we cannot verify the absence of Trojan downloaders, data exfiltration, reverse shells, or other malware logic.

The skill description assumes automatic access to multiple external systems (Odoo ERP, Supabase database, Notion task manager) for querying financial data, updating tasks, and validating permits. While some data retrieval is necessary for tax computation, the skill requests broad integration capabilities that extend beyond core form generation—such as updating project management tasks in Notion—without clear user consent mechanisms or data minimization. This could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive business systems if credentials are mishandled.
